props.message:
The Stacks.

What the Library keeps forever. Speak softly. Read in order.
Sign 1 — "The first reading is the eye that finds the page..."
Sign 2 — "...the second reading is the breath that holds it open..."
Sign 3 — "...and the third reading is the hand that carries it home."

props.open_message: You have read in the right order. Lirien sees the readers.
A glass vessel. The Stacks keep one or two of these for the long readings.

props.message:
An inscription cut deep into the wall:

"Here is kept what the Wyrdmark agreed to remember. Forgetting these things would thin the Source itself."

A stone plaque, polished by centuries of touching:

"The names of the first walkers — Vael, Anth, and the third whose name is no longer spoken."

A small wood plaque set into the floor:

"Master Archivist Tannen, who kept these stacks for sixty years and never raised her voice. Walk well."

A folded cloth notice, weighted with a stone:

"Reading aloud is permitted in the Stacks if you are alone. If you are not alone, ask first. Most readings are gentle. Some are not."

A page set under glass:

"Lirien's eye is on the page when the page is opened. The reader is, briefly, also read."

root.speaker: Master Archivist Nanre
root.text: Welcome to the Stacks, walker. Speak softly. What is here is here forever.

kept.speaker: Master Archivist Nanre
kept.text: What Lirien herself agreed should remain. Names of the founders. The shape of the Triglyph. The first inscriptions. Things that, if forgotten, would unmake the Wyrdmark.

why.speaker: Master Archivist Nanre
why.text: Because the Source remembers what is held. If the Wyrdkin forget, the Source thins where the memory used to be. The Stacks are the ballast under our walking.

cache.speaker: Master Archivist Nanre
cache.text: The Cache holds what may be lost. The Stacks hold what may not. Curator Meld and I argue, gently, about which of us has the harder craft.

bye.speaker: Master Archivist Nanre
bye.text: Walk well. Read in the right order. Lirien sees the readers.

root.speaker: Younger Archivist Rilin
root.text: Please — softer steps. I am minding the inscriptions on the east wall, and the dust here is centuries old.

choices.label: How is it studying with Nanre?

signs.speaker: Younger Archivist Rilin
signs.text: An old saying, broken into three pieces and laid in three places. A reader's test. Find them, read them in order, and the saying becomes whole. The first is at the front. The third is at the back.

nanre.speaker: Younger Archivist Rilin
nanre.text: Like studying with the wall itself. Nanre says nothing for a long time, and then a single thing that you remember for a year. I am two years in. I have remembered three things.

bye.speaker: Younger Archivist Rilin
bye.text: Walk well. Read in order, if you would.

props.message:
Pinned on a shelf of bound histories:

    All commits are equal but some commits are 'fix.'

The Stacks-keeper has read this shelf three times. The shelf is, in fact, only one commit. Long.
